items.notes: One of the most common pets in Finland. 'Koiranpentu' = puppy. Partitive: 'koiraa'
examples: Minulla on koira. — I have a dog.
Koira haukkuu. — The dog is barking.

items.notes: Very common pet. 'Kissanpentu' = kitten. Note the double 's'
examples: Kissa nukkuu sohvalla. — The cat is sleeping on the sofa.
Pidätkö kissoista? — Do you like cats?

items.notes: Stem: hevose-. The Finnish horse breed ('suomenhevonen') is a national treasure. Partitive: 'hevosta'
examples: Hevonen juoksee pellolla. — The horse is running in the field.

items.notes: Note the 'ä' at the end. Finland has a strong dairy farming tradition. Partitive: 'lehmää'
examples: Lehmät ovat laitumella. — The cows are in the pasture.

items.notes: 'Sianliha' = pork (lit: pig meat). 'Porsas' = piglet
examples: Sika on maatilan eläin. — The pig is a farm animal.

items.notes: Stem: lampaa-. This is a consonant gradation word. Plural: 'lampaat'. Partitive: 'lammasta'
examples: Lampaat laiduntavat kedolla. — The sheep are grazing in the meadow.

items.notes: Male chicken. 'Kukko' also appears in Finnish dishes like 'kalakukko' (fish-filled bread from Savonia region)
examples: Kukko kiekuu aamulla. — The rooster crows in the morning.

items.notes: General word for bird. 'Lintujen' = of birds. Finland has rich birdwatching opportunities, especially during spring migration
examples: Lintu laulaa puussa. — The bird is singing in the tree.

items.notes: 'Ankka' is also the Finnish name for Donald Duck (Aku Ankka), who is enormously popular in Finland
examples: Ankka ui järvessä. — The duck is swimming in the lake.

items.notes: The brown bear is Finland's national animal. Finns have many old euphemisms for bear: 'kontio', 'otso', 'mesikämmen' (honey paw)
examples: Karhu on Suomen kansalliseläin. — The bear is Finland's national animal.

items.notes: Stem: sude-. Wolves live in eastern and northern Finland. 'Susimetsä' = wolf forest (used metaphorically for wilderness)
examples: Susi ulvoo yöllä. — The wolf howls at night.

items.notes: The red fox is common throughout Finland. 'Ketunpoika' = fox cub. Note the double 't'
examples: Kettu on ovela eläin. — The fox is a cunning animal.

items.notes: Very common in Finland and a frequent traffic hazard. 'Hirvivaroitus' = moose warning sign. Moose hunting is a major autumn tradition
examples: Hirvi seisoo tien vieressä. — The moose is standing beside the road.

items.notes: Stem: jänikse-. Technically means hare; 'kaniini' is used for domestic rabbit. Note the 'ä'
examples: Jänis hyppii lumessa. — The hare is hopping in the snow.

items.notes: Red squirrels are common in Finnish forests and parks. The Finnish word has a pleasant, soft sound
examples: Orava kerää pähkinöitä. — The squirrel is collecting nuts.

items.notes: Iconic animal of Finnish Lapland. Semi-domesticated by the Sámi people. 'Poronhoitaja' = reindeer herder. 'Poronliha' = reindeer meat
examples: Poroja on paljon Lapissa. — There are many reindeer in Lapland.

items.notes: Stem: ilvekse-. Finland's only wild big cat. Very shy and rarely seen. The Eurasian lynx is an endangered species in Finland
examples: Ilves on harvinainen eläin. — The lynx is a rare animal.

items.notes: Stem: hylkee-. The endangered Saimaa ringed seal ('saimaannorppa') is unique to Lake Saimaa in Finland
examples: Hylje lepää kalliolla. — The seal is resting on the rock.

items.notes: Stem: perhose-. A beautiful-sounding Finnish word. Plural: 'perhoset'
examples: Perhonen lentää kukasta kukkaan. — The butterfly flies from flower to flower.

items.notes: Stem: mehiläise-. 'Mehiläishoitaja' = beekeeper. 'Hunaja' = honey. Note the 'ä' in the ending
examples: Mehiläinen pölyttää kukkia. — The bee pollinates flowers.

items.notes: Note: 'ä' appears twice. 'Hämähäkinverkko' = spider web. A fun word to practice Finnish vowel harmony
examples: Hämähäkki kutoo verkkoa. — The spider is spinning a web.

items.notes: Stem: muurahaise-. 'Muurahaispesä' = ant nest. Finland's forests are home to large wood ant colonies
examples: Muurahainen kantaa lehteä. — The ant is carrying a leaf.

items.notes: Note the double 'm' and double 'k'. Finnish frogs hibernate through the long winter
examples: Sammakko hyppää vedestä. — The frog jumps out of the water.

items.notes: Note the double 'ä' (ää). Finland's only venomous snake is the 'kyy' (European adder/viper)
examples: Käärme luikertelee ruohikossa. — The snake is slithering in the grass.

items.notes: Note the 'ö' vowels. Several owl species live in Finland, including the great grey owl ('lapinpöllö')
examples: Pöllö huhuilee metsässä. — The owl is hooting in the forest.

items.notes: The golden eagle ('maakotka') and white-tailed eagle ('merikotka') both live in Finland. 'Merikotka' is Finland's largest bird of prey
examples: Kotka lentää korkealla taivaalla. — The eagle flies high in the sky.

items.notes: The general word for animal. Stem: eläime-. 'Eläintarha' = zoo (lit: animal garden). 'Lemmikkieläin' = pet
examples: Mikä on lempieläimesi? — What is your favourite animal?
Eläimet nukkuvat talvella. — The animals sleep in winter.
